questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The director circle of a hyperbola is $x^2+y^2-4 y=0$. One end of the transverse axis is $(2,0)$ then a focus is
questions.solution: Radius of director circle $=\sqrt{\mathrm{a}^2-\mathrm{b}^2}=2$ $\begin{aligned} & (2 \sqrt{2})^2-b^2=4 \\ & b^2=4 \quad e=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{2}}\end{aligned}$ $\therefore$ Focus lies on the line $\frac{x-0}{\frac{-1}{\sqrt{2}}}=\frac{\mathrm{y}-2}{\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}}$ at a distance ae units from centre. $\therefore \frac{x-0}{-\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}}=\frac{y-2}{\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}}= \pm 2 \sqrt{3}$ $(x, y)=(\sqrt{6} .2-\sqrt{6})$ or $(-\sqrt{6}, 2+\sqrt{6})$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
options: a conic section whose eccentricity is $\sqrt{5}$
a conic whose auxillary circle is $x^2+y^2=4$
questions.solution:
It is locus of $\mathrm{P}(\mathrm{x}, \mathrm{y})$ moving such that $\left(\mathrm{PS}-\mathrm{PS}^{\prime}\right)=4 \lt \mathrm{SS}^{\prime}$, where $\mathrm{S}(4,2)$ and $S^{\prime}(-4,-2)$. So the locus is hyperbola with $S$, $\mathrm{S}^{\prime}$ as foci. Centre is mid point of $\mathrm{SS}^{\prime}$.
$2 \mathrm{a}=4,2 \mathrm{ae}=4 \sqrt{5} \Rightarrow e=\sqrt{5}$
auxiliary circle is concentric circle with radius 'a' so $x^2+y^2=4$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If two tangents can be drawn to the different branches of the hyperbola $x^2-\frac{y^2}{4}=1$ from the point $\left(\alpha, \alpha^2\right)$, then:
questions.solution: Solving $y=x^2$ and $y=2 x$ $$ \begin{aligned} & x^2=2 x \\ & x=2 \end{aligned} $$ $x>2$ or $x < -2 \Rightarrow \mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b}, \mathrm{d}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A point moves such that the sum of the squares of its distances from the two sides of length 'a' of a rectangle is twice the sum of the squares of its distances from the other two sides of length 'b'. The locus of the point can be :
questions.solution:
Let the two sides of the rectangle lie along $x$-axis \& $y$ axis as shown Given that
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow k^2+(k-b)^2=2\left(h^2+(a-h)^2\right) \\ & \Rightarrow 2 k^2-2 k b+b^2=4 h^2-4 a h+2 a^2 \end{aligned}$ Replacing h by x and k by y $\begin{aligned} \Rightarrow & 2 y^2-2 b y+b^2=4 x^2-4 a x+2 a^2 \\ & 2\left(y^2-b y\right)+b^2=4\left(x^2-a x\right)+2 a^2 \\ & 2\left(y-\frac{b}{2}\right)^2+\frac{b^2}{2}=4\left(x-\frac{a}{2}\right)^2+a^2 \\ & 4\left(x-\frac{a}{2}\right)^2-2\left(y-\frac{b}{2}\right)^2=\frac{b^2}{2}-a^2 \end{aligned}$ Hence it is a hyperbola or pair of lines if $\frac{b^2}{2}-a^2 \neq 0$ or $\frac{b^2}{2}-a^2=0$ respectively.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Circles are drawn on chords of the rectangular hyperbola $\mathrm{xy}=\mathrm{c}^2$ parallel to the line $\mathrm{y}=\mathrm{x}$ as diameters All such circles pass through two fixed points whose coordinates are $(A)(C, c)$
questions.solution:
$\frac{1}{t_1 t_2}=-1 ;\left(x-x t_1\right)\left(x-c t_2\right)+\left(y-\frac{c}{t_1}\right)\left(y-\frac{c}{t_2}\right)=1\left[t_1 t_2=-1\right]$
$\Rightarrow\left(x^2+y^2-2 c\right)-\left(t_1+t_2\right)(x-y)=0$
$\Rightarrow S-\lambda L=0$
Hence, we get (c, c) and $(-c,-c)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If the normals at $\left(x_1, y_1\right) i=1,2,3,4$ to the rectangular hyperbola $x y=2$ meet at the point $(3,4)$, then
questions.solution: Any point on $x y=2$ is $P\left(\sqrt{2} t, \frac{\sqrt{2}}{t}\right)$ Normal at $P$ is $\left(y-\frac{\sqrt{2}}{t}\right)=t^2(x-\sqrt{2} t)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If the normal at $\left(x_i, y_i\right), i=1,2,3,4$ to the rectangular hyperbola $x y=2$ meet at the point $(3,4)$, then
questions.solution:
Let the point is $P(t)$ so equation of normal at this is
$x t^3-y t=c\left(t^4-1\right)$
satisfy by $(3,4)$
so $3 \mathrm{t}^3-4 \mathrm{t}=\sqrt{2}\left(\mathrm{t}^4-1\right)\qquad$ [Given $x y=2]$
$t^4-\frac{3}{\sqrt{2}} t^3+2 \sqrt{2} t-1=0$
Here $t_1 \mathrm{t}_2 \mathrm{t}_3 \mathrm{t}_4=-1$
$\& t_1+t_2+t_3+t_4=\frac{3}{\sqrt{2}}$
But in Cartesian from $\left(x_1, y_1\right)$ is
$\begin{aligned}
& x_1=c t_1 \& y_1=\frac{c}{t_1} \\
& \frac{x_1 x_2 x_3 x_4}{c^4}=-1 \\
& x_1 x_2 x_3 x_4=-c^4=-4
\end{aligned}$
Similarly $y_1 y_2 y_3 y_4=\frac{c^4}{t_1 t_2 t_3 t_4}=\frac{4}{-1}=-4$
$\begin{aligned}
& y_1+y_2+y_3+y_4=c\left(\frac{\sum t_1 t_2 t_3}{t_1 t_2 t_3 t_4}\right)=4 \\
& x_1+x_2+x_3+x_4=c\left(t_1+t_2+t_3+t_4\right)
\end{aligned}$
$=\sqrt{2}\left(\frac{3}{\sqrt{2}}\right)=3$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Equation of common tangent(s) of $x^2-y^2=12$ and $x y=8$ is (are) -
questions.solution:
Let the common tangent is
$\begin{aligned} & y=m x \pm \sqrt{12 m^2-12} \text { solving it with } \\ & x y=8 \quad \Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{mx}^2 \pm \sqrt{12\left(\mathrm{~m}^2-1\right)} \mathrm{x}-8=0 \\ & \mathrm{D}=0 \Rightarrow 12\left(\mathrm{~m}^2-1\right)+32 \mathrm{~m}=0 \\ & 3 \mathrm{~m}^2+8 \mathrm{~m}-3=0 \Rightarrow \quad \mathrm{~m}=-3, \frac{1}{3} \\ & \mathrm{y}=-3 \mathrm{x} \pm 4 \sqrt{6} \quad \mathrm{~m}=\frac{1}{3} \quad \text { rejected }\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
